encounter_context.encounter_reason: 81-year-old woman from a skilled nursing facility presenting with urosepsis; DNR/DNI documented, baseline dementia, on chronic dialysis via AV fistula.

active_problems: Urosepsis with hypotension responsive to fluids
Acute on chronic confusion above dementia baseline
recent_changes: Blood pressure dropped to 82/50 before 500 mL fluid bolus.

medications.name: Piperacillin-tazobactam (renal-dosed)
medications.status: first dose given within 1 hour of triage
medications.why_important: Door-to-antibiotic within 1 hour is essential for sepsis mortality; renal dosing must continue.

medications.name: IV fluids
medications.status: active but cautious due to ESRD
medications.timing_or_schedule: 30 mL/kg goal held at 500 mL boluses with reassessment
medications.why_important: Volume overload risk in dialysis-dependent patient; do not reflexively give full 30 mL/kg.

procedures_and_devices: Left forearm AV fistula — DO NOT use for BP or IV access
safety_critical_facts: DNR/DNI status is documented — do not intubate or start CPR.
AV fistula on left arm is dialysis access — no BP cuffs, no IVs, no blood draws from that arm.
Give fluids in 500 mL boluses with reassessment; avoid full 30 mL/kg resuscitation due to dialysis dependence.
disposition_and_follow_up: Admit to step-down unit with nephrology on board for urgent dialysis.

supporting_source_excerpts.label: Nursing note
supporting_source_excerpts.text: Left AV fistula present; do not use left arm for BP or IV access.
